body.title: Factory template: declarative multi-agent factory with owner, orchestrator, members
body.result: factory YAML spec + FactoryInstantiator + GrannyPatternOrchestrator + factory-create skill; 39 tests; validate/instantiate CLI wired
body.description:
One level above the agent scaffold (T-agent-scaffold). A factory is a declarative YAML spec that instantiates a set of agents wired together for a problem domain. Initially always owned by a human; designed so any agent with sufficient authority can own one in the future.

Factory spec (config/factories/<name>.yaml):
  owner_id: comms://akien/          # human today, agent tomorrow
  orchestrator: granny-pattern      # one agent holds the goal, routes to members
  members:
    - agent_type: librarian
    - agent_type: scraps
    - agent_type: custom-intake     # scaffolded from template
  eval_rubric: R-factory-output     # evaluator device rubric id
  budget_limits:                    # inherited by all member agents
    daily_limit_usd: 5.00

Instantiation flow: owner approves spec → scaffold creates member agents → announce wires manifests → orchestrator receives factory goal → members route via orchestrator → health rollup reported to owner → eval scores reported to owner.

Escalation: orchestrator escalates to owner when blocked. If owner is an agent, that agent escalates up its own chain. Terminal escalation always reaches a human.

**Affected files:** config/factories/ (new), devices/installer/ (factory instantiation), devices/granny/ (orchestrator pattern reuse), skills/ (new factory-create skill)
**Design rules:** owner_id is a comms:// address — typed as agent, humans are agents on the rack (comms://akien/); no hardcoded human assumption
**Scope boundary:** IN — factory YAML spec, instantiation flow, owner approval checkpoint, health+eval rollup to owner, escalation chain; OUT — factory self-modification, cross-factory coordination
**Completion criteria:** a factory spec instantiates 2+ agents, wires their manifests, reports health to owner_id; owner receives eval scores; escalation from orchestrator reaches owner; owner can halt factory via T-agent-kill-switch
